entries.title: American Journal of Economics and Sociology X (1951, 211-219.
entries.container_title: J. Plamenatz, "The Open Society and Its Enemies", British Journal of Sociology III (1952), 264-273.

entries.title: E. H. Gombrich, "The Open Society – a Comment", British Journal of Sociology III (1952), 358-360 [On Plamenatz].
J. Plamenatz, "The Open Society – a Rejoinder", British Journal of Sociology IV (1953), 76-77.
J. D. Bernal, "Has History a Meaning?", British Journal for the Philosophy of Science VI (1955), 164-169.
Sumner, B. H., War and History, Edinburgh: Oliver and Boyd.
Zucker, Morris, The Philosophy of American History, 2 vols., New York: Arnold and Howard.
Barzun, Jacques, "As a Liberal Art", Journal of the History of Ideas VI, 81-88.
Beloff, Max, "The Study of Contemporary History: Some Further Reflections", History N. S. XXX, 75-84.
Cantimori, Delio, "Appunti sullo 'storicismo'", Società I, 1-45 (Re-printed in D. Cantimori, Studi di storia, Torino: Einau, 1959, 1-45).
Coulburn, Rushton, "The Meaning of History", Ethics LV, 46-63.
Gould, Clarence P., "History – a Science?", Mississippi Valley Review XXXII, 375-388.
Hofstadter, A., "Generality and Singularity in Historical Judgment", Journal of Philosophy XLII, 57-65.
Lenoble, R., "Les nouvelles conceptions de l'histoire", Revue philosophique XXXV, 193-208, 324-348.
McCown, C., "In History or Beyond History", Harvard Theological Review XXXVIII, 151-175.
Nichols, Roy F., "History and the Social Science Research Council", American Historical Review L, 491-499.
Popper, Karl, "The Poverty of Historicism, Part III", Economica XII, 69-89 (Parts I and II appeared in 1944. Cf. also #1233).
Weiss, Paul, "The History and the Historian", Journal of Philosophy XLII, 169-179.
White, Morton G., "The Attack on the Historical Method", Journal of Philosophy XLII, 314-331.
